Package org.eventb.core.ast.tests

Class TestSpecialization

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.AbstractTests
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.TestSpecialization
public class TestSpecialization extends AbstractTests
Unit tests about specialization creation. These tests ensure that all precondition of specialization methods are indeed implemented.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    private void
    assertEmptySpecialization()
     
    private final <T> void
    assertSet(T[] actuals, T... expected)
     
    private void
    assertSpecialization(String srcTypenvImage, String specImage, String dstTypenvImage)
     
    private void
    assertSpecialization(ISpecialization spe, String srcTypenvImage, String specImage, String dstTypenvImage)
     
    void
    testBothSwap()
    Ensures that both types and identifiers can be swapped in a specialization.
    void
    testBothSwapMixed()
    Ensures that both types and identifiers can be swapped in a specialization, entering substitutions alternatively.
    void
    testCanPut_BothSwap()
    Ensures that both types and identifiers can be swapped in a specialization.
    void
    testCanPut_BothSwapMixed()
    Ensures that both types and identifiers can be swapped in a specialization, entering substitutions alternatively.
    void
    testCanPut_DstNameConflict()
    Ensures that an expression substitute cannot conflict with the destination type environment.
    void
    testCanPut_DstNameNoConflict()
    Ensures that an expression substitute can conflict with the source type environment.
    void
    testCanPut_DstTypeConflict()
    Ensures that a type substitute cannot conflict with the destination type environment.
    void
    testCanPut_DstTypeNoConflict()
    Ensures that a type substitute can conflict with the source type environment.
    void
    testCanPut_GivenTypeIdentifier()
    Ensures that an identifier that denotes a given type is rejected, if not already registered as a type substitution.
    void
    testCanPut_GivenTypeIdentifierAlready()
    Ensures that an identifier that denotes a given type is accepted, if it has already been registered as a type substitution.
    void
    testCanPut_IdentSwap()
    Ensures that identifiers can be swapped in a specialization.
    void
    testCanPut_IllFormedExpression()
    Ensures that an identifier substitution which contains an ill-formed expression is accepted.
    void
    testCanPut_IncompatibleComplexType()
    Ensures that the type of the expression is checked within complex replacement types.
    void
    testCanPut_IncompatibleTypeNoTypeSubstitution()
    Ensures that an identifier substitution which changes type when no type substitution has been registered is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_IncompatibleTypeSubstitution()
    Ensures that a type substitution which is not compatible with the identifier substitutions is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_IncompatibleTypeWithTypeSubstitution()
    Ensures that an identifier substitution which is not compatible with the type substitution is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_NullExpression()
    Ensures that a null expression is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_NullGivenType()
    Ensures that a null given type is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_NullIdentifier()
    Ensures that a null identifier is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_NullTypeValue()
    Ensures that a null type value is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_OverriddenType()
    Ensures that a type substitution which is overridden is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_OverriddenTypeExpression()
    Ensures that a type expression substitution which is overridden is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_OverridenExpression()
    Ensures that registering two substitution for the same identifier but with different expressions is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_OverridenSameExpression()
    Ensures that registering twice the same identifier substitution is accepted.
    void
    testCanPut_OverridenSameType()
    Ensures that a inserting a type substitution identical to one already registered is accepted.
    void
    testCanPut_OverridenSameTypeExpression()
    Ensures that a inserting a type expression substitution identical to one already registered is accepted.
    void
    testCanPut_SameTypeIdentSubstitution()
    Ensures that an identifier substitution for which the replacement expression is of same type as the given identifier is accepted.
    void
    testCanPut_TypeSwap()
    Ensures that types can be swapped in a specialization.
    void
    testCanPut_UntypedExpression()
    Ensures that an untyped expression is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_UntypedIdentifier()
    Ensures that an untyped identifier is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_WrongFactoryExpression()
    Ensures that an expression from another factory is rejected.
    void
    testCanPut_WrongFactoryTypeValue()
    Ensures that a type value from another factory is rejected.
    void
    testCanPutFailure_NoSideEffect()
    Ensures that if the canPut test fails, then no substitution has been added as a side-effect.
    void
    testCanPutFailure_NoSideEffectComplexType()
    Ensures that if the canPut test fails, then no substitution has been added as a side-effect.
    void
    testCanPutSuccess_NoSideEffect()
    Ensures that if the canPut test succeeds, then no substitution has been added as a side-effect.
    void
    testCanPutSuccess_NoSideEffectComplexType()
    Ensures that if the canPut test succeeds, then no substitution has been added as a side-effect.
    void
    testDstNameConflict()
    Ensures that an expression substitute cannot conflict with the destination type environment.
    void
    testDstNameNoConflict()
    Ensures that an expression substitute can conflict with the source type environment.
    void
    testDstTypeConflict()
    Ensures that a type substitute cannot conflict with the destination type environment.
    void
    testDstTypeNoConflict()
    Ensures that a type substitute can conflict with the source type environment.
    void
    testGet_PredicateVariableWithoutSubstitution()
    Ensures that one gets null if there is no substitution and that no substitution is created as a side-effect.
    void
    testGet_PredicateVariableWithSubstitution()
    Ensures that one can retrieve a predicate varialbe substitution.
    void
    testGetFactory()
    Ensures that ISpecialization.getFactory() returns the right factory.
    void
    testGetForIdentWithoutSubstitution()
    Ensures that one gets null if there is no substitution and that no substitution is created as a side-effect.
    void
    testGetForIdentWithSubstitution()
    Ensures that one can retrieve a substitution.
    void
    testGetForIdentWithTypeSubstitution()
    Ensures that one can retrieve a substitution for an identifier corresponding to a substituted type.
    void
    testGetForTypeWithoutSubstitution()
    Ensures that one gets null if there is no substitution and that no substitution is created as a side-effect.
    void
    testGetForTypeWithSubstitution()
    Ensures that one can retrieve a substitution.
    void
    testGets()
    Ensures that types, free identifiers, and predicate variable are correctly put into the specialization.
    void
    testGivenTypeIdentifier()
    Ensures that an identifier that denotes a given type is rejected, if not already registered as a type substitution.
    void
    testGivenTypeIdentifierAlready()
    Ensures that an identifier that denotes a given type is accepted, if it has already been registered as a type substitution.
    void
    testIdentSwap()
    Ensures that identifiers can be swapped in a specialization.
    void
    testIllFormedExpression()
    Ensures that an identifier substitution which contains an ill-formed expression is accepted.
    void
    testIncompatibleComplexType()
    Ensures that the type of the expression is checked within complex replacement types.
    void

    Ensures that an identifier substitution which changes type when no type substitution has been registered is rejected.
    void
    testIncompatibleTypeSubstitution()
    Ensures that a type substitution which is not compatible with the identifier substitutions is rejected.
    void
    testIncompatibleTypeWithTypeSubstitution()
    Ensures that an identifier substitution which is not compatible with the type substitution is rejected.

    Ensures that a null expression is rejected.

    void
    testOverriddenTypeWithTypeExpression()
    Ensures that a type substitution which is overridden by a type expression substitution is rejected.
    void
    testOverridenExpression()
    Ensures that registering two substitution for the same identifier but with different expressions is rejected.
    void
    testOverridenSameExpression()
    Ensures that registering twice the same identifier substitution is accepted.
    void
    testOverridenSameType()
    Ensures that a inserting a type substitution identical to one already registered is accepted.
    void
    testOverridenSameTypeAndTypeExpression()
    Ensures that inserting a type expression substitution identical to a type substitution already registered is accepted.
    void
    testOverridenSameTypeExpression()
    Ensures that a inserting a type substitution identical to one already registered is accepted.
    void
    testOverridenSameTypeExpressionAndType()
    Ensures that inserting a type substitution identical to a type expression substitution already registered is accepted.
    void
    testPut_IllFormedPredicate()
    Ensures that a predicate variable substitution which contains an ill-formed predicate is accepted.
    void
    testPut_NullPredicateValue()
    Ensures that a null predicate value is rejected.
    void
    testPut_NullPredicateVariable()
    Ensures that a null predicate variable is rejected.
    void
    testPut_OverridenPredicate()
    Ensures that registering two substitution for the same predicate variable but with different predicates is rejected.
    void
    testPut_OverridenSamePredicate()
    Ensures that registering twice the same predicate variable substitution is accepted.
    void
    testPut_PredVarDstNameConflict()
    Ensures that a predicate substitute cannot conflict with the destination type environment.
    void
    testPut_PredVarDstNameNoConflict()
    Ensures that a predicate substitute can conflict with the source type environment.
    void
    testPut_PredVarDstTypenv()
    Ensures that adding a predicate variable substitution to a specialization adds the destination identifiers in the destination type environment.
    void
    testPut_PredVarSwap()
    Ensures that identifiers can be swapped in a specialization.
    void
    testPut_UntypedPredicate()
    Ensures that an untyped predicate is rejected.
    void
    testPut_WrongFactoryPredicateValue()
    Ensures that a typed-predicate from another factory is rejected.
    void
    testSameTypeIdentSubstitution()
    Ensures that an identifier substitution for which the replacement expression is of the same type as the given identifier is accepted.
    void
    testTypeSubstitutionNotTypeExpression()
    Ensures that a type substitution with an expression that is not a type is rejected.

      Ensures that a null given type is rejected.

      Ensures that a type value from another factory is rejected.

      Ensures that a type substitution which is overridden is rejected.

      public void testOverridenSameType()
      Ensures that a inserting a type substitution identical to one already registered is accepted.

      Ensures that a type substitute cannot conflict with the destination type environment.

      Ensures that a type substitute can conflict with the source type environment.

      public void testNullIdentifier()
      Ensures that a null identifier is rejected.

    • testUntypedIdentifier

      public void testUntypedIdentifier()
      Ensures that an untyped identifier is rejected.

    • testGivenTypeIdentifier

      public void testGivenTypeIdentifier()
      Ensures that an identifier that denotes a given type is rejected, if not already registered as a type substitution.

    • testGivenTypeIdentifierAlready

      public void testGivenTypeIdentifierAlready()
      Ensures that an identifier that denotes a given type is accepted, if it has already been registered as a type substitution.

    • testNullTypeExpressionValue

      public void testNullTypeExpressionValue()
      Ensures that a null type expression value is rejected.

    • testWrongFactoryTypeExpressionValue

      public void testWrongFactoryTypeExpressionValue()
      Ensures that a type expression value from another factory is rejected.

    • testOverriddenTypeExpression

      public void testOverriddenTypeExpression()
      Ensures that a type expression substitution which is overridden is rejected.

    • testOverriddenTypeExpressionWithType

      public void testOverriddenTypeExpressionWithType()
      Ensures that a type expression substitution which is overridden by a type substitution is rejected.

    • testOverriddenTypeWithTypeExpression

      public void testOverriddenTypeWithTypeExpression()
      Ensures that a type substitution which is overridden by a type expression substitution is rejected.

    • testOverridenSameTypeExpression

      public void testOverridenSameTypeExpression()
      Ensures that a inserting a type substitution identical to one already registered is accepted.

    • testOverridenSameTypeExpressionAndType

      public void testOverridenSameTypeExpressionAndType()
      Ensures that inserting a type substitution identical to a type expression substitution already registered is accepted.

    • testOverridenSameTypeAndTypeExpression

      public void testOverridenSameTypeAndTypeExpression()
      Ensures that inserting a type expression substitution identical to a type substitution already registered is accepted.

    • testNullGivenTypeExpression

      public void testNullGivenTypeExpression()
      Ensures that a null given type is rejected.

    • testUntypedTypeExpression

      public void testUntypedTypeExpression()
      Ensures that an untyped type expression is rejected.

    • testTypeSubstitutionNotTypeExpression

      public void testTypeSubstitutionNotTypeExpression()
      Ensures that a type substitution with an expression that is not a type is rejected.

    • testNullExpression

      public void testNullExpression()
      Ensures that a null expression is rejected.

    • testWrongFactoryExpression

      public void testWrongFactoryExpression()
      Ensures that an expression from another factory is rejected.

    • testUntypedExpression

      public void testUntypedExpression()
      Ensures that an untyped expression is rejected.

    • testIncompatibleTypeNoTypeSubstitution

      public void testIncompatibleTypeNoTypeSubstitution()
      Ensures that an identifier substitution which changes type when no type substitution has been registered is rejected. Moreover, there is no side-effect performed during the check.

    • testIncompatibleTypeWithTypeSubstitution

      public void testIncompatibleTypeWithTypeSubstitution()
      Ensures that an identifier substitution which is not compatible with the type substitution is rejected.

    • testIncompatibleComplexType

      public void testIncompatibleComplexType()
      Ensures that the type of the expression is checked within complex replacement types.

    • testSameTypeIdentSubstitution

      public void testSameTypeIdentSubstitution()
      Ensures that an identifier substitution for which the replacement expression is of the same type as the given identifier is accepted. Moreover, the given sets occurring in the type shall also be added.

    • testOverridenExpression

      public void testOverridenExpression()
      Ensures that registering two substitution for the same identifier but with different expressions is rejected.

    • testOverridenSameExpression

      public void testOverridenSameExpression()
      Ensures that registering twice the same identifier substitution is accepted.

    • testIncompatibleTypeSubstitution

      public void testIncompatibleTypeSubstitution()
      Ensures that a type substitution which is not compatible with the identifier substitutions is rejected.

    • testIllFormedExpression

      public void testIllFormedExpression()
      Ensures that an identifier substitution which contains an ill-formed expression is accepted.

    • testTypeSwap

      public void testTypeSwap()
      Ensures that types can be swapped in a specialization.

    • testIdentSwap

      public void testIdentSwap()
      Ensures that identifiers can be swapped in a specialization.

    • testBothSwap

      public void testBothSwap()
      Ensures that both types and identifiers can be swapped in a specialization.

    • testBothSwapMixed

      public void testBothSwapMixed()
      Ensures that both types and identifiers can be swapped in a specialization, entering substitutions alternatively.

    • testDstNameConflict

      public void testDstNameConflict()
      Ensures that an expression substitute cannot conflict with the destination type environment.

    • testDstNameNoConflict

      public void testDstNameNoConflict()
      Ensures that an expression substitute can conflict with the source type environment.

    • testGetFactory

      public void testGetFactory()
      Ensures that ISpecialization.getFactory() returns the right factory.

      public void testGetForTypeWithSubstitution()
      Ensures that one can retrieve a substitution.

    • testGetForTypeWithoutSubstitution

      public void testGetForTypeWithoutSubstitution()
      Ensures that one gets null if there is no substitution and that no substitution is created as a side-effect.

    • testGetForIdentWithSubstitution

      public void testGetForIdentWithSubstitution()
      Ensures that one can retrieve a substitution.

    • testGetForIdentWithTypeSubstitution

      public void testGetForIdentWithTypeSubstitution()
      Ensures that one can retrieve a substitution for an identifier corresponding to a substituted type.

    • testGetForIdentWithoutSubstitution

      public void testGetForIdentWithoutSubstitution()
      Ensures that one gets null if there is no substitution and that no substitution is created as a side-effect.

      Ensures that a null given type is rejected.

      Ensures that a null type value is rejected.

      Ensures that if the canPut test fails, then no substitution has been added as a side-effect.

    • testPut_IllFormedPredicate

      public void testPut_IllFormedPredicate()
      Ensures that a predicate variable substitution which contains an ill-formed predicate is accepted.

    • testPut_PredVarSwap

      public void testPut_PredVarSwap()
      Ensures that identifiers can be swapped in a specialization.

    • testPut_PredVarDstTypenv

      public void testPut_PredVarDstTypenv()
      Ensures that adding a predicate variable substitution to a specialization adds the destination identifiers in the destination type environment.

    • testPut_PredVarDstNameConflict

      public void testPut_PredVarDstNameConflict()
      Ensures that a predicate substitute cannot conflict with the destination type environment.

    • testPut_PredVarDstNameNoConflict

      public void testPut_PredVarDstNameNoConflict()
      Ensures that a predicate substitute can conflict with the source type environment.

    • testGet_PredicateVariableWithSubstitution

      public void testGet_PredicateVariableWithSubstitution()
      Ensures that one can retrieve a predicate varialbe substitution.

    • testGet_PredicateVariableWithoutSubstitution

      public void testGet_PredicateVariableWithoutSubstitution()
      Ensures that one gets null if there is no substitution and that no substitution is created as a side-effect.

    • testGets

      public void testGets()
      Ensures that types, free identifiers, and predicate variable are correctly put into the specialization.

    • assertSet

      @SafeVarargs private final <T> void assertSet(T[] actuals, T... expected)

    • assertEmptySpecialization

      private void assertEmptySpecialization()

    • assertSpecialization

      private void assertSpecialization(String srcTypenvImage, String specImage, String dstTypenvImage)

    • assertSpecialization

      private void assertSpecialization(ISpecialization spe, String srcTypenvImage, String specImage, String dstTypenvImage)
